children.heading: Regulations
children.label: a Regulations
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agencies shall prescribe uniform regulations effective , which prohibit any out-of-State bank from using any authority to engage in interstate branching pursuant to this title, or any amendment made by this title to any other provision of law, primarily for the purpose of deposit production.June 1, 199711See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Guidelines for meeting credit needs
children.label: b Guidelines for meeting credit needs
children.text: Regulations issued under subsection (a) shall include guidelines to ensure that interstate branches operated by an out-of-State bank in a host State are reasonably helping to meet the credit needs of the communities which the branches serve.

children.label: A
children.text: the appropriate Federal banking agency for the out-of-State bank shall review the loan portfolio of the bank and determine whether the bank is reasonably helping to meet the credit needs of the communities served by the bank in the host State; and

children.label: i
children.text: the agency may order that an interstate branch or branches of such bank in the host State be closed unless the bank provides reasonable assurances to the satisfaction of the appropriate Federal banking agency that the bank has an acceptable plan that will reasonably help to meet the credit needs of the communities served by the bank in the host State, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the out-of-State bank may not open a new interstate branch in the host State unless the bank provides reasonable assurances to the satisfaction of the appropriate Federal banking agency that the bank will reasonably help to meet the credit needs of the community that the new branch will serve.

children.label: A
children.text: whether the interstate branch or branches of the out-of-State bank were formerly part of a failed or failing depository institution;

children.label: B
children.text: whether the interstate branch was acquired under circumstances where there was a low loan-to-deposit ratio because of the nature of the acquired institution’s business or loan portfolio;

children.label: C
children.text: whether the interstate branch or branches of the out-of-State bank have a higher concentration of commercial or credit card lending, trust services, or other specialized activities;

children.label: D
children.text: the ratings received by the out-of-State bank under the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 [ et seq.];12 U.S.C. 2901

children.label: E
children.text: economic conditions, including the level of loan demand, within the communities served by the interstate branch or branches of the out-of-State bank; and

children.label: F
children.text: the safe and sound operation and condition of the out-of-State bank.

children.heading: Notice required
children.label: A Notice required
children.text: Before exercising any authority under paragraph (1)(B)(i), the appropriate Federal banking agency shall issue to the bank a notice of the agency’s intention to close an interstate branch or branches and shall schedule a hearing.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: B Hearing
children.text: shall apply to any proceeding brought under this paragraph.Section 1818(h) of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: d Application
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to any interstate branch established or acquired in a host State pursuant to this title or any amendment made by this title to any other provision of law.11

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency, bank, State, and State bank
children.label: 1 Appropriate Federal banking agency, bank, State, and State bank
children.text: The terms “appropriate Federal banking agency”, “bank”, “State”, and “State bank” have the same meanings as in .section 1813 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a national bank, the State in which the main office of the bank is located; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a State bank, the State by which the bank is chartered.

children.heading: Host State
children.label: 3 Host State
children.text: The term “host State” means a State in which a bank establishes a branch other than the home State of the bank.

children.heading: Interstate branch
children.label: 4 Interstate branch
children.text: The term “interstate branch” means a branch established pursuant to this title or any amendment made by this title to any other provision of law and any branch of a bank controlled by an out-of-State bank holding company (as defined in section 1841()(7) of this title).11o

children.heading: Out-of-State bank
children.label: 5 Out-of-State bank
children.text: The term “out-of-State bank” means, with respect to any State, a bank the home State of which is another State and, for purposes of this section, includes a foreign bank, the home State of which is another State.
